patterns.question: What is ShadowScan?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan is a Shadow Copy management system for Cursor workspaces. It automatically creates backups when you open files, restores them on close, and provides tools to view changes, undo modifications, and manage backup history.

patterns.question: How does ShadowScan work?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan creates shadow copies of your files when you open them in Cursor. It stores these copies in .shadow-scan/backups/ directory. When you close files, it can restore them to their original state. You can view diffs, selectively undo changes, and manage backup history through the Backup Manager UI.

patterns.question: Is ShadowScan safe to use?
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan is safe. It creates backups before making changes and never modifies your original files without confirmation. All restore operations require explicit user approval. The system uses MD5 hashing to verify file integrity.

patterns.question: What is Auto-Backup on Open?
patterns.normalized: what is auto backup on open
patterns.answer: Auto-Backup on Open automatically creates a shadow copy of a file when you first open it in Cursor. This ensures you always have a restore point before making any changes. The backup is stored in .shadow-scan/backups/ with a timestamp.

patterns.question: What is Auto-Restore on Close?
patterns.normalized: what is auto restore on close
patterns.answer: Auto-Restore on Close automatically restores files to their original state when you close them in Cursor. This is useful for temporary edits or when you want to discard all changes made during a session.

patterns.question: How do I detect changes in my files?
patterns.answer: Use the 'Detect Changes' command or click 'View Diff' in the notification. ShadowScan compares your current file against its shadow copy and shows you exactly what lines were added, removed, or modified.

patterns.question: What is View Diff?
patterns.answer: View Diff opens a side-by-side comparison showing your current file on the right and the original shadow copy on the left. Changes are highlighted: green for additions, red for deletions, and yellow for modifications.

patterns.question: How do I undo specific changes?
patterns.normalized: how do i undo specific changes
patterns.answer: Click 'Selectively Undo Changes' to see a list of individual modifications. Check the boxes next to the changes you want to revert, then confirm. Only the selected changes will be undone while keeping other modifications.

patterns.question: How do I revert all changes?
patterns.normalized: how do i revert all changes
patterns.answer: Click 'Fully Revert to Original' to restore the file to its shadow copy state. A confirmation dialog will appear showing the file path and timestamp. Type 'RESTORE' to confirm or 'n' to cancel.

patterns.question: What is the Backup Manager?
patterns.answer: The Backup Manager is a UI panel (accessible via Ctrl+Shift+B) that lets you view, search, restore, and delete your backup history. It shows backups organized by timestamp with file paths and sizes.

patterns.question: How do I schedule backups?
patterns.answer: Open the Backup Manager and click 'Schedule Backups'. You can set intervals (5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes). Scheduled backups run automatically in the background while Cursor is open.

patterns.question: What is backup health monitoring?
patterns.answer: Backup Health Monitoring tracks the integrity of your backups using MD5 checksums. It detects corrupted backups, missing files, and storage issues. You can view health status in the Backup Manager sidebar.

patterns.question: How do cleanup policies work?
patterns.answer: Cleanup policies automatically remove old backups based on rules you set. Options include: keep last N backups, delete backups older than X days, or keep only backups from the last week. Configure in Backup Manager settings.

patterns.question: What is restore policy?
patterns.answer: Restore Policy defines what happens when you restore a backup. Options include: overwrite current file, create a new file with suffix, merge changes, or ask each time. Configure in Backup Manager settings.

patterns.question: How do I search my backups?
patterns.normalized: how do i search my backups
patterns.answer: Use the Searchable Backup Index in the Backup Manager. You can search by filename, date range, file type, or content. Results show matching backups with timestamps and file sizes.

patterns.question: What is Smart Restore?
patterns.answer: Smart Restore intelligently handles conflicts when restoring backups. If your current file has changes different from the backup, it offers options: overwrite, merge compatible changes, or create a conflict file for manual review.

patterns.question: How do I give feedback on responses?
patterns.answer: After receiving a response, you'll see helpful/not helpful buttons. Click the appropriate button to rate the response. This feedback helps improve future responses and trains the system.

patterns.question: What is auto-training?
patterns.answer: Auto-Training allows the support bot to learn from interactions. When enabled, it can accept or reject suggested responses based on user feedback, improving accuracy over time.

patterns.question: Error: Backup directory not found
patterns.answer: This error occurs when the .shadow-scan/backups/ folder doesn't exist. Solution: Run 'Initialize ShadowScan' from the command palette, or manually create the directory structure. Check that your workspace has write permissions.

patterns.question: Error: Cannot read file permissions
patterns.answer: This error means ShadowScan can't access the file due to permission issues. Solution: Check file permissions, ensure the file isn't locked by another process, and verify Cursor has read access to the workspace.

patterns.question: Error: MD5 hash mismatch
patterns.answer: MD5 hash mismatch indicates the backup file may be corrupted. Solution: Delete the corrupted backup and create a new one. The system will automatically create a fresh backup on next file open.

patterns.question: Error: Backup storage full
patterns.answer: Backup storage is full. Solution: Run cleanup in Backup Manager to remove old backups, or manually delete files from .shadow-scan/backups/. Consider enabling automatic cleanup policies.

patterns.question: Error: Failed to restore backup
patterns.answer: Restore failed due to file conflicts or permission issues. Solution: Check if the file is open in another program, verify write permissions, and try using Smart Restore for conflict resolution.

patterns.question: Error: Shadow scan index corrupted
patterns.answer: The backup index database is corrupted. Solution: Delete .shadow-scan/index.json and restart ShadowScan. It will rebuild the index from existing backup files.

patterns.question: Error: Backup already exists for this timestamp
patterns.normalized: error backup already exists for this timestamp
patterns.answer: A backup with this timestamp already exists. Solution: Wait a moment and try again, or manually delete the existing backup before creating a new one.

patterns.question: Error: Cannot connect to backup server
patterns.normalized: error cannot connect to backup server
patterns.answer: Connection to backup server failed. Solution: Check your network connection, verify server settings in ShadowScan config, and ensure firewall isn't blocking the connection.

patterns.question: How do I enable ShadowScan?
patterns.answer: To enable ShadowScan: 1) Open Cursor, 2) Press Ctrl+Shift+P, 3) Type 'ShadowScan: Initialize', 4) Press Enter. The system will create necessary directories and start monitoring files.

patterns.question: How do I disable ShadowScan?
patterns.answer: To disable ShadowScan: 1) Open Cursor, 2) Press Ctrl+Shift+P, 3) Type 'ShadowScan: Disable', 4) Press Enter. Existing backups will be preserved but no new backups will be created.

patterns.question: How do I open the Backup Manager?
patterns.normalized: how do i open the backup manager
patterns.answer: Open Backup Manager with: 1) Press Ctrl+Shift+B, or 2) Click the Shield icon in the activity bar, or 3) Press Ctrl+Shift+P and type 'ShadowScan: Open Backup Manager'.

patterns.question: How do I view file history?
patterns.normalized: how do i view file history
patterns.answer: To view file history: 1) Right-click a file in Explorer, 2) Select 'ShadowScan: View History', 3) Browse through timestamps and versions. You can also search by date range in Backup Manager.

patterns.question: How do I restore a specific backup?
patterns.normalized: how do i restore a specific backup
patterns.answer: To restore a specific backup: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Find the backup by date/time, 3) Click 'Restore' button, 4) Confirm the restore operation. The file will be restored to its state at that backup time.

patterns.question: How do I delete old backups?
patterns.normalized: how do i delete old backups
patterns.answer: To delete old backups: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Select backups to delete, 3) Click 'Delete' button, 4) Confirm deletion. You can also enable automatic cleanup policies to handle this.

patterns.question: How do I compare two backups?
patterns.normalized: how do i compare two backups
patterns.answer: To compare two backups: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Select two backups using checkboxes, 3) Click 'Compare' button, 4) View side-by-side diff. Changes between the two versions will be highlighted.

patterns.question: How do I backup all files?
patterns.normalized: how do i backup all files
patterns.answer: To backup all files: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Click 'Backup All' button, 3) Select files to include, 4) Click 'Create Backups'. All selected files will be backed up immediately.

patterns.question: Why are my backups not being created?
patterns.normalized: why are my backups not being created
patterns.answer: Backups may not be created if: 1) ShadowScan is disabled, 2) File permissions prevent writing, 3) Backup directory is missing, 4) Storage is full. Check Backup Manager status and ensure all prerequisites are met.

patterns.question: Why is the diff view not showing changes?
patterns.normalized: why is the diff view not showing changes
patterns.answer: Diff view may not show changes if: 1) No backup exists for the file, 2) File hasn't been modified, 3) Changes are identical to backup. Create a backup first, then make changes to see the diff.

patterns.question: Why is restore failing silently?
patterns.answer: Silent restore failure can occur due to: 1) File locked by another process, 2) Insufficient permissions, 3) Disk space issues. Check file locks, run as administrator if needed, and verify disk space.

patterns.question: Why is the Backup Manager not opening?
patterns.normalized: why is the backup manager not opening
patterns.answer: Backup Manager may not open if: 1) Extension is disabled, 2) UI components failed to load, 3) Conflicting extensions. Try reloading window (Ctrl+Shift+P > Reload Window) or disabling conflicting extensions.

patterns.question: Why are scheduled backups not running?
patterns.normalized: why are scheduled backups not running
patterns.answer: Scheduled backups may not run if: 1) Scheduling is disabled, 2) Cursor is in background, 3) Timer is paused. Check Backup Manager settings, ensure Cursor is active, and verify schedule configuration.

patterns.question: How do I fix corrupted backups?
patterns.normalized: how do i fix corrupted backups
patterns.answer: To fix corrupted backups: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Check health status, 3) Delete corrupted backups, 4) Create new backups. The system will detect and flag corrupted files automatically.

patterns.question: Can ShadowScan backup large files?
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan can backup files of any size. However, very large files (100MB+) may take longer to backup and use more storage. Consider using cleanup policies to manage storage for large files.

patterns.question: Where are backups stored?
patterns.answer: Backups are stored in the .shadow-scan/backups/ directory within your workspace. Each backup includes the file content, metadata, and MD5 checksum. The directory structure mirrors your workspace folders.

patterns.question: How much storage do backups use?
patterns.normalized: how much storage do backups use
patterns.answer: Storage usage depends on file sizes and backup frequency. Each backup stores the full file content. Use cleanup policies to limit storage. You can view storage usage in Backup Manager settings.

patterns.question: Does ShadowScan work with Git?
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan works alongside Git. It creates backups independently of Git commits. You can use both systems - Git for version control and ShadowScan for quick local restore points.

patterns.question: Can I backup binary files?
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan can backup binary files including images, compiled files, and other non-text content. The backup preserves the exact binary content.

patterns.question: rever changes
patterns.answer: Did you mean 'revert changes'? To revert changes: 1) Click 'Fully Revert to Original' in the notification, 2) Confirm by typing 'RESTORE', 3) Or use 'Selectively Undo Changes' for specific modifications.

patterns.question: undo chnages
patterns.answer: Did you mean 'undo changes'? To undo changes: 1) Use 'Selectively Undo Changes' for specific modifications, 2) Or 'Fully Revert to Original' to restore the entire file.

patterns.question: bakup not working
patterns.answer: Did you mean 'backup not working'? Check: 1) ShadowScan is enabled, 2) Backup directory exists, 3) File permissions are correct, 4) Storage space is available. See Troubleshooting section for detailed steps.

patterns.question: how to shcedule backups
patterns.answer: Did you mean 'how to schedule backups'? Open Backup Manager (Ctrl+Shift+B), click 'Schedule Backups', select interval (5/15/30/60 minutes), and enable automatic backups.

patterns.question: storgae full error
patterns.answer: Did you mean 'storage full error'? Solution: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Run cleanup to remove old backups, 3) Enable automatic cleanup policies, 4) Check .shadow-scan/backups/ size.

patterns.question: What happens if I delete the .shadow-scan folder?
patterns.normalized: what happens if i delete the shadow scan folder
patterns.answer: Deleting the .shadow-scan folder removes all backups. ShadowScan will recreate the folder structure on next use, but previous backups are lost. Always backup important files before deleting this folder.

patterns.question: Can I use ShadowScan in multiple workspaces?
patterns.normalized: can i use shadowscan in multiple workspaces
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan works independently in each workspace. Each workspace has its own .shadow-scan folder with separate backups. Configure settings per workspace as needed.

patterns.question: Does ShadowScan slow down Cursor?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan has minimal impact on Cursor performance. Backups are created asynchronously and don't block the editor. Very large files may cause brief pauses during backup creation.

patterns.question: How do I export my backups?
patterns.normalized: how do i export my backups
patterns.answer: To export backups: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Select backups to export, 3) Click 'Export', 4) Choose export location. Backups are exported as compressed archives that can be imported later.

patterns.question: How do I import backups?
patterns.answer: To import backups: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Click 'Import', 3) Select the backup archive, 4) Confirm import. Imported backups will appear in your backup history.

patterns.question: Why is selective undo not working?
patterns.normalized: why is selective undo not working
patterns.answer: Selective undo may fail if: 1) No changes are detected, 2) File was modified since backup, 3) Changes are conflicting. Try fully reverting or creating a new backup first.

patterns.question: Why is my backup not in the list?
patterns.normalized: why is my backup not in the list
patterns.answer: A backup may not appear if: 1) It was deleted by cleanup policy, 2) Index is corrupted, 3) Backup creation failed. Check health status and recreate the backup if needed.

patterns.question: Can I backup only changed lines?
patterns.normalized: can i backup only changed lines
patterns.answer: Currently, ShadowScan creates full file backups. Differential/incremental backups are planned for a future version. Full backups ensure complete restore capability.

patterns.question: How do I report a bug?
patterns.normalized: how do i report a bug
patterns.answer: To report a bug: 1) Open Backup Manager, 2) Click 'Report Issue', 3) Describe the problem, 4) Include error messages if any, 5) Submit. You can also report issues on our GitHub repository.

patterns.question: How do I contact support?
patterns.answer: To contact support: 1) Use this support bot for instant help, 2) Open Backup Manager > Help > Contact Support, 3) Email support@shadowscan.dev, 4) Visit our documentation at docs.shadowscan.dev.

patterns.question: What are the keyboard shortcuts?
patterns.answer: Key shortcuts: Ctrl+Shift+B (Backup Manager), Ctrl+Shift+D (Detect Changes), Ctrl+Shift+R (Restore), Ctrl+Z (Undo in diff view). Check Keybindings in settings for all shortcuts.

patterns.question: How do I update ShadowScan?
patterns.answer: To update ShadowScan: 1) Open Extensions panel (Ctrl+Shift+X), 2) Find ShadowScan, 3) Click 'Update' if available, 4) Restart Cursor. Backups are preserved during updates.

patterns.question: Can I backup .env files?
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan can backup .env files. However, be aware that backups may contain sensitive information. Consider excluding .env from backups or using encrypted backups for security.

patterns.question: How do I exclude files from backup?
patterns.normalized: how do i exclude files from backup
patterns.answer: To exclude files: 1) Open ShadowScan settings, 2) Add patterns to 'Exclude List', 3) Use glob patterns like *.log or node_modules/**. Excluded files won't be backed up.

patterns.question: What is the maximum number of backups?
patterns.normalized: what is the maximum number of backups
patterns.answer: There's no hard limit on backup count. However, storage space and cleanup policies determine how many backups are retained. Configure cleanup policies to manage backup count automatically.

patterns.question: Is it safe?
patterns.answer: Yes, ShadowScan is safe. It creates backups before making any changes and never modifies your files without confirmation. All operations require explicit user approval. You can revert any changes using the Revert feature.

patterns.question: What does this tool do?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan cleans your PC and checks for security threats. It removes temp files, finds orphaned folders, scans for malware, optimizes startup, and repairs system files. All changes are backed up so you can revert if needed.

patterns.question: What features does it have?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan has 17 features: Temp cleanup, Downloads cleanup, Orphan detection, Accessibility fixes, WiFi optimization, Shadow account removal, Startup cleanup, Registry cleanup, System optimization, Process scanning, System repair, Startup analyzer, Service analyzer, Browser cleaning, Visual effects, Developer cache cleanup, and WinSxS cleanup.

patterns.question: Can I undo changes?
patterns.answer: Yes! ShadowScan backs up all changes before making them. Use the Revert feature to undo any changes. You can revert everything or select specific categories to revert.

patterns.question: How do I run it?
patterns.answer: Right-click Run-Cleanup.bat and select 'Run as Administrator'. Or run in PowerShell: .\ShadowScan.ps1 -All -DryRun (preview) or .\ShadowScan.ps1 -All (execute).

patterns.question: What does it clean?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan cleans temp files, old downloads, orphaned folders, browser cache, developer caches (npm, pip, VS Code), registry entries, and WinSxS components. It also scans for malware and optimizes startup.

patterns.question: Is it free?
patterns.answer: Yes! ShadowScan CLI is completely free and open source under the MIT license. The Pro edition with GUI and extra features is $19 one-time purchase.

patterns.question: What is the pro version?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan Pro is the $19 one-time purchase edition. It includes everything in the free CLI plus a GUI wrapper, scheduled scans, auto-updates, and detailed reports.

patterns.question: How long does it take?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan typically takes 5-15 minutes for most PCs. The SFC/DISM repair can take 10-30 minutes. Use -DryRun first to preview without making changes.

patterns.question: Does it delete my files?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan only deletes temp files, old downloads, and browser cache. Your documents, photos, and important files are never touched. Use -DryRun to preview what will be deleted.

patterns.question: What Windows versions are supported?
patterns.answer: ShadowScan works on Windows 10 and Windows 11. It requires PowerShell 5.1 or later.

patterns.question: Do I need admin rights?
patterns.answer: Admin rights are recommended for full cleanup (registry, services, system repair). Some features work without admin, but you'll get the best results with administrator privileges.

patterns.question: Will this kill my computer?
patterns.answer: No! ShadowScan is completely safe. It creates backups before any changes and never modifies critical system files. You can revert everything if needed. Use -DryRun first to preview changes without making them.
